FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Reactive

Namespace
FluentGpu.Signals
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

The reactive core: a Solid/Preact-style fine-grained reactivity graph. Signals are observable cells; computations (effects, memos, component render-effects) auto-subscribe to the signals they READ during a run, and re-run when a read signal changes. This is the single update mechanism the whole engine is built on — a property binding is an effect at node granularity; a component re-render is an effect at subtree granularity.

Threading: UI-thread-confined (the engine's single render/reconcile thread), so tracking state is ThreadStaticAttribute. Scheduling is DEFERRED: a signal write marks dependent effects stale and asks the host for a frame; the host drains them once per frame via Flush() (phase 3), which keeps reconcile/layout on their phases and the per-frame paint allocation-free.

public static class Reactive

Methods

OnCleanup(Action)

Register a cleanup to run when the enclosing computation re-runs or is disposed (the Solid onCleanup).

public static void OnCleanup(Action cleanup)

Parameters

cleanup Action

Untrack(Action)

Read a signal-backed value without subscribing (peek).

public static void Untrack(Action fn)

Parameters

fn Action

Untrack<T>(Func<T>)

Run fn without subscribing the current computation to any signal it reads.

public static T Untrack<T>(Func<T> fn)

Parameters

fn Func<T>

Returns

T

T
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
